title: Full Auth.authenticate/2 refactor preserving error distinctions
prompt: Refactor `MyApp.Auth.authenticate/2` from test_fixtures/nested_case_auth.ex to use a `with` expression. Preserve error distinctions: `{:error, :invalid_credentials}` for missing user OR bad password (these must be indistinguishable from outside), `{:error, reason}` pass-through for Session + Token failures. Add an `else` clause only where actually needed — DO NOT create a complex-else anti-pattern with one clause per possible error. Instead, normalize error shapes at the clause boundary so `with` can pass them through. Output a complete `defmodule MyApp.Auth do ... end` including the needed helpers.
tier_rationale: Must normalize error shapes at the clause boundary to avoid the complex-else anti-pattern. Subtle tradeoff.
research_citation: Elixir Streams (German Velasco): refactoring complex-else clauses by extracting per-step helpers
